title: Smoky Lamb Pot Roast
excerpt: Ingredients 1.2 kg thick-cut lamb shanks 250 ml well-seasoned flour olive oil to taste 2 large, finely chopped onions 2 large carrots, cut into medium rings 4 large celery stalks, cut into medium slices 2 red bell peppers, ribs removed and diced 4 – 6 large finely chopped or grated garlic cloves (or to taste) …

seo.title: Smoky Lamb Pot Roast - Marinda Kook

html:
Ingredients

  • 1.2 kg thick-cut lamb shanks
  • 250 ml well-seasoned flour
  • olive oil to taste
  • 2 large, finely chopped onions
  • 2 large carrots, cut into medium rings
  • 4 large celery stalks, cut into medium slices
  • 2 red bell peppers, ribs removed and diced
  • 4 – 6 large finely chopped or grated garlic cloves (or to taste)
  • 15 ml smoked paprika
  • 2 bay leaves (preferably fresh)
  • 250 ml prepared lamb or beef stock
  • 2 x 410 g cans chopped tomatoes
  • salt, freshly ground black pepper and sugar to taste
  • dried red chilli flakes (optional and to taste)
  • extra garlic and smoked paprika, if you want to add more later
  • chopped parsley to taste

Method

Dip the meat lightly in the flour and then fry a few pieces at a time in an oven-proof pot in which you've heated a little oil.

Remove the browned meat and set aside.

Heat a bit more oil in the pot and add the vegetables.

Sauté until the vegetables are fragrant, then add the garlic, paprika and bay leaves.

Stir well and pour in the stock and canned tomatoes.

Put the meat into the pot and season to taste with salt, black pepper and sugar. Also add, to taste, dried red chilli flakes.

Bring to the boil and then place the pot on a large baking tray.

Bake in the oven, preheated to 190°C, for 90 minutes or until the meat is tender. If there's still too much liquid in the pot, remove the lid, raise the oven heat slightly and bake further until the sauce has reduced enough to your taste.

Remove the pot from the oven, season further as desired with extra seasoning as well as garlic and smoked paprika if you feel it's needed.

Stir through the parsley and serve with any starch of your choice: soft polenta, creamy mashed potatoes, Italian gnocchi, rice or pasta.

Serves 6

Serve with a green salad.

Easy!
